claim: A December 25, 2002 email exchange between Cheryl Mills — longtime counsel to Bill and Hillary Clinton, later Counselor and Chief of Staff at the State Department under Secretary Clinton — and Ghislaine Maxwell was released by the U.S. Department of Justice as part of the Epstein-related document productions to the U.S. House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform in 2025. In the exchange, Maxwell informs Mills that it is her birthday; Mills replies, in part, “Hate you for being in Palm Beach” — Palm Beach being where Jeffrey Epstein owned his Florida home. Mills's relationship to Maxwell, predating Maxwell's 2020 arrest and 2021 sex-trafficking conviction, was not previously a matter of public record at this level of granularity.

sources.label: NewsNation — “Epstein files: Clinton's lawyer Cheryl Mills named in Ghislaine Maxwell DOJ deposition release.”
U.S. House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform — Epstein document production releases (2025).

notes: The Mills/Maxwell email surfaced as part of the second batch of Epstein-related documents the Department of Justice produced to the House Oversight Committee in 2025. Mills was Bill Clinton's deputy counsel during the impeachment proceedings, served as Counselor and Chief of Staff at State under Hillary Clinton, and later represented Clinton during the email-server investigation. The email's date (Christmas Day 2002) is independently consistent with Maxwell's known birthday.
